description: Functions and classes for working with calendars, including generating text and HTML calendars for months or years.

functions.name: calendar

params.name: year
params.description: The year to generate a calendar for

params.name: w
params.description: Width of date columns. Default is 2.

params.name: l
params.description: Number of lines per week. Default is 1.

params.name: c
params.description: Number of spaces between month columns. Default is 6.

returns.description: A multi-line string containing the formatted calendar for the entire year

functions.description: Returns a multi-line string with a calendar for the entire year, formatted across three columns. Uses the first weekday as set by setfirstweekday().

functions.name: month

params.name: w
params.description: Width of date columns. Default is 0 (auto).

params.name: l
params.description: Number of lines per week. Default is 0 (auto).

returns.description: A multi-line string containing the formatted month calendar

functions.description: Returns a multi-line string with a calendar for the given month of the given year.

functions.name: monthcalendar

returns.description: A list of week lists. Each week list contains 7 ints (day numbers, 0 for days outside the month).

functions.description: Returns a matrix (list of lists) representing a month's calendar. Each row represents a week; days outside the month are set to 0.

functions.name: monthrange

returns.description: A tuple (weekday_of_first_day, number_of_days_in_month). Weekday is 0=Monday through 6=Sunday.

functions.description: Returns the weekday of the first day of the month (0=Monday) and the number of days in the month for the specified year and month.

functions.name: weekday

returns.description: The day of the week (0=Monday through 6=Sunday)

functions.description: Returns the day of the week for the given date. 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday.

functions.name: isleap

returns.description: True if the year is a leap year, False otherwise

functions.description: Returns True if the given year is a leap year, according to the proleptic Gregorian calendar.

functions.name: leapdays

returns.description: The number of leap years in the range

functions.description: Returns the number of leap years in the range [y1, y2), useful for computing the number of days between two year-based dates.

functions.name: setfirstweekday

params.name: weekday
params.description: The day to set as the first day of the week (0=Monday through 6=Sunday). Use the constants MONDAY, TUESDAY, etc.

returns.description: No return value

functions.description: Sets the first day of the week (0=Monday, 6=Sunday) for all calendar-generation functions. The default is Monday.

functions.name: firstweekday

returns.description: The current setting for the first weekday (0=Monday through 6=Sunday)

functions.description: Returns the current setting for the weekday that starts each week.

functions.name: timegm

params.name: tuple
params.description: A time tuple in UTC (as returned by time.gmtime())

returns.description: Seconds since the epoch (Unix timestamp)

functions.description: An unrelated but handy function that takes a time tuple in UTC and returns the corresponding Unix timestamp. This is the inverse of time.gmtime().

functions.name: prmonth

params.name: w
params.description: Width of date columns. Default is 0 (auto).

params.name: l
params.description: Number of lines per week. Default is 0 (auto).

returns.description: No return value; prints to stdout

functions.description: Prints the calendar for a month. Like calendar.month() but prints the result to stdout instead of returning a string.

functions.name: prcal

params.name: w
params.description: Width of date columns. Default is 2.

params.name: l
params.description: Number of lines per week. Default is 1.

params.name: c
params.description: Number of spaces between month columns. Default is 6.

returns.description: No return value; prints to stdout

functions.description: Prints the calendar for an entire year. Like calendar.calendar() but prints the result to stdout instead of returning a string.

classes.name: Calendar
classes.description: Base class for calendar generation. Provides iterator and matrix methods for building calendars. Subclassed by TextCalendar and HTMLCalendar.

params.name: firstweekday
params.description: The first day of the week (0=Monday, 6=Sunday). Default is 0.

constructors.description: Creates a Calendar object with the given first weekday.

methods.name: iterweekdays

returns.description: An iterator over weekday numbers for one week, starting with firstweekday

methods.description: Returns an iterator for the week day numbers used in one week. The first value from the iterator will be the same as the firstweekday property.

methods.name: itermonthdates

returns.description: An iterator of datetime.date objects for all days in the month, plus fill days to complete weeks

methods.description: Returns an iterator for the month, yielding datetime.date objects for all days and fill days before and after the month needed to complete full weeks.

methods.name: itermonthdays

returns.description: An iterator of day numbers (1-31) for the month, with 0 for fill days

methods.description: Returns an iterator for the month, similar to itermonthdates(), but yields day numbers instead of datetime.date objects. Days outside the month are represented by 0.

methods.name: itermonthdays2

returns.description: An iterator of (day_number, weekday_number) tuples

methods.description: Returns an iterator for the month, yielding tuples of (day_number, weekday_number). Days outside the month have day_number 0.

methods.name: monthdatescalendar

returns.description: A list of weeks; each week is a list of 7 datetime.date objects

methods.description: Returns a list of the weeks in the month as lists of 7 datetime.date objects.

methods.name: monthdays2calendar

returns.description: A list of weeks; each week is a list of 7 (day_number, weekday_number) tuples

methods.description: Returns a list of the weeks in the month as lists of 7 (day_number, weekday_number) tuples. Days outside the month have day_number 0.

methods.name: monthdayscalendar

returns.description: A list of weeks; each week is a list of 7 day numbers (0 for fill days)

methods.description: Returns a list of the weeks in the month as lists of 7 day numbers. Days outside the month are represented by 0.

methods.name: yeardatescalendar

params.name: width
params.description: Number of months per row. Default is 3.

returns.description: A list of month rows; each row contains width months; each month is a list of weeks; each week is a list of 7 datetime.date objects

methods.description: Returns the data for the entire year as a list of month rows. Each row contains up to width months (default 3). Each month contains weeks as lists of datetime.date objects.

classes.name: TextCalendar
classes.description: Subclass of Calendar that generates plain-text calendars. Can format single months or full years as strings or print them to stdout.

params.name: firstweekday
params.description: The first day of the week (0=Monday, 6=Sunday). Default is 0.

constructors.description: Creates a TextCalendar with the given first weekday.

methods.name: formatmonth

returns.description: A multi-line string with the formatted month calendar

methods.description: Returns a month's calendar as a multi-line string.

methods.name: formatyear

params.name: w
params.description: Width of date columns. Default is 2.

params.name: c
params.description: Spacing between month columns. Default is 6.

params.name: m
params.description: Number of months per row. Default is 3.

returns.description: A multi-line string with the formatted year calendar

methods.description: Returns a full year's calendar as a multi-line string with m months per row.

methods.name: prmonth

returns.description: No return value; prints to stdout

methods.description: Prints a month's calendar to stdout.

methods.name: pryear

returns.description: No return value; prints to stdout

methods.description: Prints a full year's calendar to stdout.

classes.name: HTMLCalendar
classes.description: Subclass of Calendar that generates HTML-formatted calendars. Can output individual months, full years, or complete HTML pages.

params.name: firstweekday
params.description: The first day of the week (0=Monday, 6=Sunday). Default is 0.

constructors.description: Creates an HTMLCalendar with the given first weekday.

methods.name: formatmonth

params.name: withyear
params.description: If True, the year is included in the header. Default is True.

returns.description: An HTML table string representing the month calendar

methods.description: Returns a month's calendar as an HTML table.

methods.name: formatyear

params.name: width
params.description: Number of months per row. Default is 3.

returns.description: An HTML table string representing the full year calendar

methods.description: Returns a full year's calendar as an HTML table.

methods.name: formatyearpage

params.name: width
params.description: Number of months per row. Default is 3.

params.name: css
params.description: Filename for the CSS stylesheet. Default is 'calendar.css'. None disables the link.

params.name: encoding
params.description: Character encoding for the HTML output. Default is system default.

returns.description: A complete HTML page string with the year calendar

methods.description: Returns a full year's calendar as a complete HTML page, including DOCTYPE, head, and CSS link.

constants.name: MONDAY
constants.description: Constant for Monday (value 0). Use with setfirstweekday().

constants.name: TUESDAY
constants.description: Constant for Tuesday (value 1). Use with setfirstweekday().

constants.name: WEDNESDAY
constants.description: Constant for Wednesday (value 2). Use with setfirstweekday().

constants.name: THURSDAY
constants.description: Constant for Thursday (value 3). Use with setfirstweekday().

constants.name: FRIDAY
constants.description: Constant for Friday (value 4). Use with setfirstweekday().

constants.name: SATURDAY
constants.description: Constant for Saturday (value 5). Use with setfirstweekday().

constants.name: SUNDAY
constants.description: Constant for Sunday (value 6). Use with setfirstweekday().

constants.name: day_name
constants.description: A list of weekday names in the current locale, e.g. ['Monday', 'Tuesday', ..., 'Sunday'].

constants.name: day_abbr
constants.description: A list of abbreviated weekday names in the current locale, e.g. ['Mon', 'Tue', ..., 'Sun'].

constants.name: month_name
constants.description: A list of month names in the current locale. month_name[0] is an empty string. month_name[1] is 'January', etc.

constants.name: month_abbr
constants.description: A list of abbreviated month names in the current locale. month_abbr[0] is an empty string. month_abbr[1] is 'Jan', etc.
